title: Hieracium leoninum
intro: Hieracium leoninum, also known as lion's spittle or devil's nettle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Hieracium leoninum contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which are used to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Hieracium leoninum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, known as Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, a large group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. This classification is based on various characteristics, including its leaves, stems, and flower structure. Hieracium leoninum is native to certain regions and can be found growing in a variety of environments. The plant's classification is used to identify and distinguish it from other similar species within the Asteraceae family.

It has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The plant's properties are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its medicinal benefits. However, the plant's medicinal uses should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
